questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "Buy pure and natural honey of company X." - an advertisement in a newspaper.

choices.b: People do not mind paying more for pure and natural honey.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Detergents should be used to clean clothes.

choices.b: Detergents help to dislodge grease and dirt.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "We need more clerks in our office staff." - The manager said to the chairman.

choices.b: The present office staff is inefficient.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "If I am not well, you will have to go for the meeting." - A manager tells his subordinate.

choices.a: It is not necessary that only manager-level personnel attend the meeting.
choices.b: If the manager is well, he would himself like to go for the meeting.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: To pass the examination, you have to practice very hard.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "He son never attends office by 8 p.m., inform on phone at home." - The parents tell their son.

choices.a: The son never informs about his late-coming.
choices.b: Unless specified, the son may not inform his parents.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "Use aluminium - the versatile metal for packaging." - an advertisement.

choices.a: Aluminium is the only versatile metal.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The President assured the people that elections will be held there every five years.

choices.a: People are afraid that the elections may not be held at all.
choices.b: People are afraid that elections may not be held after five years.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: These apples are too good.

choices.a: When the apple crop is abundant, the prices go down.
choices.b: The lower the selling price, the inferior the quality of the commodity.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: If it is easy to become an engineer, I don't want to be an engineer.

choices.a: An individual aspires to be a professional.
choices.b: One desires to achieve a thing which is hard-earned.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: This book is so prepared that even a layman can study science in the absence of a teacher.

choices.a: A layman wishes to study science without a teacher.
choices.b: This book is a good substitute for a teacher.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: If you have any problems, bring them to me.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Over 1/3rd lakh quintals of cotton has been procured in the state under Cotton Procurement Scheme, a press note said.

choices.b: No cotton procurement should be done now.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: More commuters now travel by this route, but there is no public demand for more buses.

choices.a: The number of buses depends upon the number of passengers.
choices.b: Usually people do not tolerate inconvenience.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: You know that your suit is excellent when people ask about your tailor who tailored the suit.

choices.a: People do not ask about your tailor if your suit is not good.
choices.b: People want to know the criterion of an excellent suit.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: All existing inequalities can be reduced, if not totally eradicated, by action of governments or by revolutionary change of government.

choices.b: No person would voluntarily part with what he possesses.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Neither fascism nor communism has any chance of succeeding in America.

choices.a: American people are strongly in favour of preserving the rights of the individual.
choices.b: Americans have so far not suffered any pangs of poverty or deprivation.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "In order to bring punctuality in our office, we must provide conveyance allowance to our employees." - The incharge of a company tells the Personnel Manager.

choices.a: Conveyance allowance will not help in bringing punctuality.
choices.b: Discipline and reward should always go hand in hand.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: A warning in a train compartment - "To stop train, pull the chain. Penalty for misuse: Rs. 500."

choices.a: Some people misuse the alarm chain.
choices.b: On certain occasions, people may want to stop a running train.
